title: Recent Reversal in China's Steel Production
section_path: Recent Reversal in China's Steel Production
section_path_text: Recent Reversal in China's Steel Production
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
Daily crude steel production at large and medium-sized mills in China most recently averaged 2.06 million tons during March 1 - March 10. This is 3% lower than was seen during late February, is down year-on-year by 4%, and marks the lowest production average seen since late January. Image reference: $1.jpg | assets/2024-03-20_recent-reversal-in-chinas-steel-production_img_241_745381a60906.jpg
Overall, Commodore Research remains of its view that the 2.13 million ton production average seen in late February could easily end up being this year's peak.
